steps.title: Apply the standard circle equation
steps.content:
This is the standard form for a circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$. The radius is squared on the right side.

Given center $(16, 17)$ and radius $7k$:
$$(x - 16)^2 + (y - 17)^2 = (7k)^2$$

steps.title: Simplify the squared radius
steps.content:
We must square the entire radius, including the variable:
$$(7k)^2 = 7^2 \cdot k^2 = 49k^2$$
The circle equation is:
$$(x - 16)^2 + (y - 17)^2 = 49k^2$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
